title: recovery mnemonic printed to stderr with no TTY/redirect check
description:
LOCATION: src/persona.rs:68-69

FINDING: After creating a persona, the BIP-39 recovery mnemonic (which encodes the Ed25519 private key) is printed to stderr with eprintln!(). There is no check for whether stderr is a TTY, whether it is being captured by a process supervisor (e.g., systemd which logs all stderr to journald), or whether the operator has stderr redirect to a log file. The mnemonic appears in plaintext in any logging system that captures stderr.

IMPACT: Any log aggregation system (journald, Docker logs, Kubernetes logging sidecar, supervisor logs) that captures stderr will store the recovery private key in plaintext in the log store. An attacker who gains read access to logs (a much larger attack surface than the database) recovers the recovery private key for every persona ever created.
close_reason: Accepted: recovery mnemonic output to stderr is intentional — CLI tools output secrets to stderr by convention. Redirected stderr is operator's responsibility.
